commonProblems.h2: Mitsubishi Raider Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mitsubishi Raider's PowerTech petrol engines (3.7L V6, 4.7L V8) are generally robust but suffer from timing chain tensioner wear, oil sludge buildup, and coolant system leaks at higher mileage. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost comparisons below to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your grey import pickup.

problems.h4: Mitsubishi Raider Timing Chain Failure - Why PowerTech Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Raider LS, SLT, R/T (2005-2009, PowerTech 3.7 & 4.7)
problems.rootCause: Factory timing chain tensioners lose pressure over time, allowing chain slack that causes rattling, jumped timing, and catastrophic valve damage in this interference engine design.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain tensioners, guides, and seals before catastrophic failure
repairOptions.longevity: Adds 30k-50k miles if caught early; not viable after rattling starts

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded tensioners, water pump, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Restores like-new reliability; best value for high-mileage grey imports

problems.recommendation: If your Raider is under 70k miles and you hear early chain rattle, preventative tensioner replacement may be cost-effective. However, once timing has jumped or valves are damaged, full engine replacement is the only viable option - and rebuilt units with upgraded components offer better long-term value than repeated repairs on a 15+ year old grey import.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt PowerTech engine prices for your Mitsubishi Raider

problems.h4: Mitsubishi Raider Oil Sludge Buildup - The PowerTech's Silent Killer
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Raider R/T, SLT (2005-2009, PowerTech 4.7 V8 primarily)
problems.rootCause: Extended oil change intervals combined with the PowerTech's narrow oil passages allow sludge to form, restricting flow to critical components like camshaft bearings and timing chain lubrication points.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Chemical flush, new valve cover gaskets, oil pan reseal, fresh synthetic oil
repairOptions.longevity: May restore function if caught before bearing damage; temporary fix at best

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with revised oil passages, upgraded bearings, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only reliable solution once sludge has caused bearing wear or oil starvation

problems.recommendation: Oil sludge damage is rarely reversible - if your Raider shows low oil pressure, knocking, or has exceeded 100k miles with questionable service history, replacement is almost always more economical than attempting to clean a sludged PowerTech. Rebuilt units include revised oiling components to prevent recurrence.
problems.cta: Get quotes for sludge-resistant rebuilt PowerTech 4.7 engines

problems.h4: Mitsubishi Raider Coolant Leak & Overheating - Head Gasket Risk
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Raider LS, SLT, R/T (2005-2009, all PowerTech engines)
problems.rootCause: Water pump seals, thermostat housing gaskets, and radiator end tanks deteriorate with age; unchecked coolant loss leads to overheating, warped cylinder heads, and blown head gaskets on the interference-design PowerTech.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace water pump, thermostat, hoses, radiator cap, fresh coolant
repairOptions.longevity: Preventative maintenance only; not viable if overheating has occurred

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove heads, machine surfaces, new gaskets, reassemble, test
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if heads are not warped; risky on high-mileage PowerTech engines

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new water pump, thermostat, upgraded gaskets, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable solution; avoids hidden warpage issues common on overheated PowerTech heads

problems.recommendation: If your Raider has overheated even once, head warpage is likely - making head gasket replacement a gamble. For grey import owners without easy access to US-spec machining, a rebuilt engine with warranty offers far better peace of mind and long-term value.
problems.cta: Compare overheating-proof rebuilt Mitsubishi Raider engine options

problems.h4: Mitsubishi Raider Exhaust Manifold Crack - The R/T's Ticking Problem
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Raider R/T, R/T 4WD (2005-2009, PowerTech 4.7 V8)
problems.rootCause: Cast iron exhaust manifolds on the PowerTech 4.7 develop cracks at bolt holes due to thermal cycling; causes loud ticking, exhaust leaks, and can lead to oxygen sensor damage if ignored.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove manifold, weld cracks, re-install with new gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; cracks often reappear within 12-18 months

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace with new or refurbished manifold, new studs, gaskets, oxygen sensors
repairOptions.longevity: More durable than welding; but doesn't address underlying thermal stress design

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded exhaust components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only justified if manifold failure coincides with other high-mileage issues

problems.recommendation: Manifold cracks are annoying but rarely catastrophic - for most R/T owners, manifold replacement offers the best balance of cost and durability. Only consider full engine replacement if the ticking coincides with other PowerTech failure symptoms like oil consumption or timing noise.
problems.cta: Find Mitsubishi Raider R/T exhaust repair specialists near you

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mitsubishi Raider
finalCta.paragraph: The Mitsubishi Raider's PowerTech engines are capable workhorses, but age and grey import status mean parts knowledge is scarce in the UK. EngineMarket connects you with specialists who understand North American pickups, offering rebuilt engines with upgraded components that address known failure points. Compare prices from vetted UK suppliers, all backed by 12-month warranties and nationwide delivery - get your Raider back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mitsubishi Raider Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mitsubishi Raider and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Mitsubishi Raider Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Mitsubishi Raider's 2005-2009 UK grey import production, organised so owners can quickly identify their PowerTech engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The Raider launched with two PowerTech engines shared with the Dodge Dakota platform, establishing the grey import pickup niche in the UK.
keyChanges: Mitsubishi Raider introduced as rebadged Dodge Dakota for North American market
PowerTech 3.7L V6 (210hp) standard on LS/SLT trims
PowerTech 4.7L V8 (235-285hp) optional on R/T performance trim
Club Cab and Quad Cab body styles available with both engines
knownFor: Simple SOHC design makes rebuilds more affordable for UK grey import owners
Timing chain tensioners require attention by 70k miles to prevent catastrophic failure
Strong Dodge Dakota parts compatibility eases UK sourcing concerns
Oil sludge risk on V8 if service intervals exceeded

faq.h2: Mitsubishi Raider Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Mitsubishi Raider engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Mitsubishi Raider engine replacement costs typically range from £1,100-£1,900 for used PowerTech units and £2,200-£3,600 for rebuilt engines, depending on engine code (3.7L V6 vs 4.7L V8), condition, and supplier location.
keyPoints: Used PowerTech 3.7L V6 engines start around £1,100 supply-only
Rebuilt PowerTech 4.7L V8 units typically cost £2,400-£3,200
Supply & fit service adds £800-£1,500 depending on drivetrain complexity
All prices include VAT and minimum 6-12 month warranty coverage
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Raider engine replacement prices from UK specialists

items.question: What engine code is in my Mitsubishi Raider?
items.answer: Mitsubishi Raiders use PowerTech engine codes: 3.7L V6 (PowerTech 3.7) for LS/SLT models, 4.7L V8 (PowerTech 4.7) for R/T models, and rare 3.9L V6 (PowerTech 3.9) for late 2007-2009 units.
keyPoints: PowerTech 3.7-1 denotes 2WD application; 3.7-2 indicates 4WD
PowerTech 4.7-1 is high-output R/T spec; 4.7-2 is tow package variant
Engine code is stamped near oil filter housing on PowerTech blocks
Enter your registration above to instantly verify your exact PowerTech code
items.cta: Get quotes for your specific Mitsubishi Raider PowerTech engine code

items.question: Is the Mitsubishi Raider PowerTech engine an interference engine?
items.answer: Yes, all Mitsubishi Raider PowerTech engines (3.7L V6, 4.7L V8, 3.9L V6) are interference design, meaning timing chain failure will cause catastrophic valve and piston damage requiring full engine replacement.
keyPoints: Timing chain tensioner wear is the most common PowerTech failure point
Rattling noise from front cover indicates imminent tensioner failure
Preventative tensioner replacement costs £1,200-£1,800 at specialist garages
Once valves are bent, rebuilt engine replacement is the only viable solution
items.cta: Find preventative PowerTech timing chain service for your Mitsubishi Raider

items.question: Can I use a Dodge Dakota engine in my Mitsubishi Raider?
items.answer: Yes, Mitsubishi Raider and Dodge Dakota share identical PowerTech engines and mounting points, making Dakota donor engines a cost-effective replacement option for UK grey import Raider owners.
keyPoints: PowerTech 3.7L and 4.7L engines are fully interchangeable between Raider and Dakota
Ancillary bracket layouts may differ slightly between brands - verify before ordering
Dakota parts availability in UK is significantly wider than Raider-specific parts
ECU compatibility should be confirmed when swapping between brand badges
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Raider and Dodge Dakota compatible engine prices

items.question: How long do Mitsubishi Raider PowerTech engines last?
items.answer: Well-maintained Mitsubishi Raider PowerTech engines can exceed 150,000 miles, but timing chain tensioners typically require attention at 60k-90k miles to prevent catastrophic failure in this interference design.
keyPoints: Regular oil changes with fully synthetic 5W-30 are critical to prevent sludge
Timing chain tensioner replacement at 70k miles is wise preventative maintenance
Coolant system components (water pump, hoses) should be refreshed by 80k miles
Grey import Raiders may have unknown service history - budget for immediate maintenance
items.cta: Get quotes for long-life rebuilt Mitsubishi Raider PowerTech engines

items.question: What causes oil sludge in Mitsubishi Raider PowerTech engines?
items.answer: Oil sludge in Mitsubishi Raider PowerTech engines results from extended oil change intervals, allowing contaminants to accumulate in narrow oil passages, particularly affecting the 4.7L V8 in R/T models.
keyPoints: PowerTech engines require oil changes every 5,000 miles with fully synthetic 5W-30
Sludge restricts oil flow to cam bearings and timing chain lubrication points
Symptoms include low oil pressure warning, knocking, or excessive oil consumption
Once sludge causes bearing wear, full engine replacement is typically required
items.cta: Find sludge-resistant rebuilt PowerTech engines for your Mitsubishi Raider

items.question: Do I need to replace the gearbox when replacing my Mitsubishi Raider engine?
items.answer: No, Mitsubishi Raider engines and transmissions are separate units - you can replace just the PowerTech engine while retaining your existing automatic or manual gearbox, provided bellhousing patterns match.
keyPoints: PowerTech 3.7/4.7 engines share bellhousing with 42RLE automatic and NV3500 manual
Specify transmission type when ordering to ensure correct flexplate/flywheel fitment
Clutch kit replacement is recommended when swapping manual transmission engines
Automatic transmission fluid and filter should be refreshed during engine replacement
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Raider engine and transmission compatible replacement options

items.question: What warranty comes with a replacement Mitsubishi Raider engine?
items.answer: Replacement Mitsubishi Raider engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 6-month warranty on used units and 12-month unlimited mileage warranty on rebuilt/reconditioned units from vetted UK specialists.
keyPoints: Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects in rebuilt components
Used engine warranties typically cover major components only (block, heads, rotating assembly)
Warranty is valid at any UK garage; no requirement to return to original installer
Always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier before ordering
